title: requests — permissions
description: Permission strings this module publishes. Seeded by `AccessRequestPermissionSeeder` with `is_system=true`. Split by guard: `sanctum` for tenant users; `platform_admin` for Stackra cross-tenant ops.

permissions.name: access-requests.viewAny
permissions.description: List access requests where the caller participates (as requester, approver, or resource owner). Seeded on every user — access requests are self-service by design.

permissions.name: access-requests.view
permissions.description: Show a specific access request. Same participation-based visibility.

permissions.name: access-requests.create
permissions.description: Submit a new access request. Seeded on every user by default — the whole point of the module is self-service.

permissions.name: access-requests.withdraw
permissions.description: Withdraw your own pending request. Seeded on every user.

permissions.name: access-requests.approve
permissions.description: Approve a request where you're in the resolved approvers set. Seeded on every user — approver membership is data-driven (via workflow/approvals selectors + access/delegation), not seeded.

permissions.name: access-requests.reject
permissions.description: Reject a request where you're in the resolved approvers set.

permissions.name: access-requests.manage_any_tenant
permissions.description: Tenant-admin scope. Grants visibility over every access request in the tenant, regardless of participation. Does NOT confer approve/reject authority — that still comes from the approver-resolver set.

permissions.name: access-requests.manage_any
permissions.description: Cross-tenant read for Stackra ops (abuse investigation, compliance reporting, support tickets). Scoped to a small platform-admin role tier.

notes: The seededOn: ["*"] entries are seeded by USER auto-provisioning — every fresh User in every tenant gets these 6 permissions on the sanctum guard. That's the whole point: access requests are a substrate every authenticated user can reach.

There is INTENTIONALLY no separate `access-requests.list_own` vs `access-requests.list_reviewing` split — both come through the single `viewAny` gate; the CONTROLLER decides which subset to return based on the route (`GET /access-requests` filters to caller's own; `GET /access-requests/pending-my-review` filters to caller-as-approver). Splitting the permission strings would let admins ban a user from seeing their own requests, which never made sense.

Approver membership itself is NOT permission-based — it's derived from workflow/approvals selectors + delegation state at write time. So `access-requests.approve` is a shape check (`this operation exists at all for this user`), not an authorization gate.
